steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The notes assign apatite a Mohs hardness of 5, calcite a 3, and topaz an 8. A higher Mohs number means a harder mineral that can scratch minerals with a lower number. The correct choice must accurately reflect apatite's position relative to both calcite and topaz.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A: does not correctly express apatite's comparative hardness relative to both other minerals. ✗
  • Choice B: leaves out or misrepresents apatite's position in the hardness ordering. ✗
  • Choice C: does not capture the relative hardness relationship among all three minerals. ✗
  • Choice D: states that apatite (5) is harder than calcite (3) but softer than topaz (8), directly matching the Mohs scale data and the scratch-ability rule. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
